name: Maceda – Baños de Molgas Loop
name_original: Maceda – vuelta por Baños de Molgas
description: This difficult 22.6-mile mountain biking route near Maceda offers challenging terrain, 1603 feet of elevation gain, and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Maceda, located in the province of Ourense, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by a mix of forests, mountains, and historical sites, providing varied terrain for mountain bike trails. The surrounding Ourense province further enhances the appeal with facilities like Bike Park Manzaneda, situated in the Serra de Queixa, which offers a range of trails. This area provides a rich environment for mountain biking within a landscape of natural beauty and historical significance. Best mountain bike trails around Maceda The most popular mountain bike trail is Maceda – Baños de Molgas Loop, a 22.6 miles (36.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Maceda – Loop through Bidueiral de Mont, a difficult 32.2 miles (51.8 km) path. This route explores forested areas and offers extensive views of the region. Local mountain bikers also love the Singletrack Descent loop from Esgos, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Maceda is defined by diverse landscapes, historical attractions, and natural features such as forests and mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Maceda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 159 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Maceda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Maceda?
A: There are nearly 40 mountain bike trails around Maceda, offering a good mix of moderate and challenging routes for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Maceda?
A: Mountain bike trails in Maceda feature diverse landscapes, including forested areas, mountainous terrain, and varied natural features. You'll encounter a mix of challenging ascents and descents, often with extensive views of the region.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Maceda?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are a couple of easier mountain bike trails available around Maceda. These routes are generally shorter and have less demanding elevation changes, making them more suitable for families or those new to mountain biking.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along mountain bike routes in Maceda?
A: Many routes in the Maceda area pass by significant historical and natural landmarks. You might encounter the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4012022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa Cristina&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, which is carved into natural rock. There are also several viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cividade Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Maceda?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Maceda are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31475709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Santa Mariña loop from Estación de Ponte Ambía&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 45.5 km route that offers a great circular experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Maceda?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of historical sites with natural beauty, and the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Maceda?
A: Maceda offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. While summer can be warm, the forested routes offer shade. Winter riding is possible, but check local weather conditions for rain or colder temperatures.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Maceda has several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38165636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Singletrack Descent loop from Esgos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 60.8 km trail with significant elevation changes, often taking over 6 hours to complete, making it ideal for experienced riders seeking a demanding adventure.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in Maceda?
A: While specific parking facilities for each trailhead are not detailed, Maceda and its surrounding towns generally offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local maps or information for designated parking areas close to your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer extensive views?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994122891/maceda-loop-through-bidueiral-de-mont" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maceda – Loop through Bidueiral de Mont&lt;/a&gt; explore forested areas and provide extensive views of the region, allowing you to appreciate the natural beauty of Ourense province.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Maceda?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the trail's difficulty. Moderate routes might have a few hundred meters of ascent, while difficult trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro de Rocas Trail loop from Alto do Couso&lt;/a&gt; can feature over 1300 meters of elevation gain, providing a substantial workout.

faqs.question: Is the Maceda – vuelta por Baños de Molgas trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this mountain bike route is considered difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It requires good physical fitness and cycling experience due to its technical aspects, significant elevation changes, and challenging surfaces like rocky sections and loose terrain. Riders should be prepared for steep inclines and technical descents, and may even need to dismount and push their bikes in certain sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on the Maceda – vuelta por Baños de Molgas route is challenging and varied. You can expect rocky sections, loose ground, and technical singletrack. There are also steep climbs and descents, with some parts potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Maceda – vuelta por Baños de Molgas mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 36.3 kilometers long with an elevation gain of about 489 meters. While the estimated riding time is around 3 hours, given its 'difficult' grading and technical sections, experienced mountain bikers should plan for a total duration of 4 to 6 hours, allowing for breaks and challenging sections.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Maceda – vuelta por Baños de Molgas is designed as a loop route, starting and ending in the same general area, likely Maceda.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not available, Maceda and Baños de Molgas are likely starting points. It's advisable to look for public parking areas within these towns, especially near the presumed start of the loop, such as the area around the Riverside Walk of Maceda or the Castillo de Maceda.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include the scenic Riverside Walk of Maceda, the spiritual site of Os Milagros, and the historic Muiño de Dositeo. You'll also pass through the beautiful Arnoia Gorges – Mills Route between Baños de Molgas and Acea, and potentially near the impressive Castillo de Maceda.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Ourense region generally offers picturesque landscapes. To enjoy the trail's challenging nature and scenery, spring and autumn are typically the best seasons, offering milder temperatures and more stable weather conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions, making the technical terrain even more demanding.

faqs.question: Is the Maceda – vuelta por Baños de Molgas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail are not provided, mountain biking trails in Galicia often allow dogs. However, given the route's 'difficult' rating, technical terrain, and potential for rocky sections, it might be very challenging or even unsafe for dogs, especially without proper training and gear. If you do bring a dog, it should be well-behaved, on a leash, and capable of handling strenuous conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to ride the Maceda – vuelta por Baños de Molgas mountain bike route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Alto da Breña, Ponte de Francos, and chaira trails, though these make up a small percentage of the overall route.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the trail?
faqs.answer: Both Maceda and Baños de Molgas are towns where you can find cafes, pubs, and other establishments to get refreshments or food before or after your ride. It's recommended to check local listings for current operating hours.
